title: Form basics
description: form, input, button — the three-part harmony.

questions.prompt: Which attribute of <form> sets where to submit?
questions.explanation: action=URL. Method defaults to GET; use method="post" for form submissions with side effects.

questions.prompt: For a button INSIDE a form to submit it, what type should it have?
questions.explanation: The default for <button> in a form is submit — but being explicit avoids surprises.

questions.prompt: The user types 'ada' and presses Enter. What key/value gets submitted?
questions.explanation: The input's name becomes the key. Default method is GET → /go?user=ada.

questions.prompt: This button reloads the page instead of submitting. Fix it.
questions.hint: type="button" makes it a plain button — no form submission.
questions.explanation: For submit behavior, use type="submit" or omit type (default is submit).
